stmt: ‚ąÄ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ‚Ąā V] (bc : BosonCore V) (n : ‚Ąē), bc.number (bc.ket n) = (n : ‚Ąā) ‚ÄĘ bc.ket n
‚ąÄ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ‚Ąā V] (bc : BosonCore V) (n : ‚Ąē), bc.number * bc.a ^ n - bc.a ^ n * bc.number = (- (n : ‚Ąā)) ‚ÄĘ bc.a ^ n
‚ąÄ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ‚Ąā V] (bc : BosonCore V), bc.vacuum ‚Č† 0 ‚Üí LinearIndependent ‚Ąā (fun n : ‚Ąē => bc.ket n)
‚ąÄ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ‚Ąā V] (bc : BosonCore V) (n : ‚Ąē), bc.number * bc.aDagger ^ n - bc.aDagger ^ n * bc.number = (n : ‚Ąā) ‚ÄĘ bc.aDagger ^ n
‚ąÄ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ‚Ąā V] (bc : BosonCore V) {v : V} {őľ : ‚Ąā} (n : ‚Ąē), bc.number v = őľ ‚ÄĘ v ‚Üí bc.number ((bc.aDagger ^ n) v) = (őľ + (n : ‚Ąā)) ‚ÄĘ ((bc.aDagger ^ n) v)

‚ąÄ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ‚Ąā V] (bc : BosonCore V) (n : ‚Ąē), bc.number * bc.aDagger ^ (n + 1) - bc.aDagger ^ (n + 1) * bc.number = (bc.number * bc.aDagger ^ n - bc.aDagger ^ n * bc.number) * bc.aDagger + bc.aDagger ^ n * (bc.number * bc.aDagger - bc.aDagger * bc.number)

No nontrivial finite-dimensional complex vector space admits a bosonic core: for every `V`, if `[AddCommGroup V] [Module ‚Ąā V] [FiniteDimensional ‚Ąā V] [Nontrivial V]`, then there does not exist any `bc : BosonCore V`.

For every bosonic core `bc` with `bc.vacuum ‚Č† 0`, every eigenvector `v` of `bc.number` with eigenvalue `őľ` generates an infinite family of pairwise linearly independent eigenvectors under repeated creation, provided none of them vanish: if `bc.number v = őľ ‚ÄĘ v` and `(bc.aDagger ^ n) v ‚Č† 0` for all `n : ‚Ąē`, then the family `fun n : ‚Ąē => (bc.aDagger ^ n) v` is linearly independent over `‚Ąā`.
‚ąÄ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ‚Ąā V] (bc : BosonCore V) {v : V} {őľ : ‚Ąā} (n : ‚Ąē), bc.number v = őľ ‚ÄĘ v ‚Üí bc.number ((bc.a ^ n) v) = (őľ - (n : ‚Ąā)) ‚ÄĘ ((bc.a ^ n) v)

For every bosonic core `bc`, the `‚Ąā`-submodule `Submodule.span ‚Ąā (Set.range bc.ket)` is invariant under both ladder operators: if `v ‚ąą Submodule.span ‚Ąā (Set.range bc.ket)`, then `bc.a v ‚ąą Submodule.span ‚Ąā (Set.range bc.ket)` and `bc.aDagger v ‚ąą Submodule.span ‚Ąā (Set.range bc.ket)`.

For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and `‚Ąā`-submodule `W ‚Č§ V`, if `bc.vacuum ‚Č† 0`, `bc.vacuum ‚ąą W`, and `W` is invariant under both `bc.a` and `bc.aDagger`, then `W` is not finite-dimensional over `‚Ąā`.

‚ąÄ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ‚Ąā V] (bc : BosonCore V) (m n : ‚Ąē), bc.number * (bc.aDagger ^ m * bc.a ^ n) - (bc.aDagger ^ m * bc.a ^ n) * bc.number = ((m : ‚Ąā) - (n : ‚Ąā)) ‚ÄĘ (bc.aDagger ^ m * bc.a ^ n)

For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and `‚Ąā`-submodule `W ‚Č§ V`, if `bc.vacuum ‚ąą W` and `‚ąÄ v ‚ąą W, bc.aDagger v ‚ąą W`, then `Submodule.span ‚Ąā (Set.range bc.ket) ‚Č§ W`.

For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and `‚Ąā`-submodule `W ‚Č§ V`, if `bc.vacuum ‚ąą W`, `‚ąÄ v ‚ąą W, bc.a v ‚ąą W`, and `‚ąÄ v ‚ąą W, bc.aDagger v ‚ąą W`, then the restrictions of `bc.a` and `bc.aDagger` to `W`, together with `bc.vacuum` viewed as an element of `W`, define a bosonic core structure on `W`.

For every complex vector space V, bosonic core bc : BosonCore V, and vector v : V, if bc.vacuum ‚Č† 0 and v ‚ąą Submodule.span ‚Ąā (Set.range bc.ket), then there exists a unique finitely supported coefficient function l : ‚Ąē ‚Üí‚āÄ ‚Ąā such that Finsupp.linearCombination ‚Ąā (fun n : ‚Ąē => bc.ket n) l = v.

For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, `Submodule.span ‚Ąā (Set.range bc.ket)` is equal to the intersection of all `‚Ąā`-submodules `W ‚Č§ V` such that `bc.vacuum ‚ąą W` and `‚ąÄ v : V, v ‚ąą W ‚Üí bc.aDagger v ‚ąą W`.

For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and polynomial `p : Polynomial ‚Ąā`, evaluating `p` at `bc.aDagger` on the vacuum is equal to the polynomial support-sum `p.sum (fun n z => z ‚ÄĘ bc.ket n)`.

For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, let `S := Submodule.span ‚Ąā (Set.range bc.ket)`. Then there exists a bosonic core `bcS : BosonCore S` such that for every `‚Ąā`-submodule `W ‚Č§ V` with `bc.vacuum ‚ąą W`, `‚ąÄ v : V, v ‚ąą W ‚Üí bc.a v ‚ąą W`, and `‚ąÄ v : V, v ‚ąą W ‚Üí bc.aDagger v ‚ąą W`, there exists `bcW : BosonCore W` for which the inclusion map `S ‚Ü™ W` sends `bcS.vacuum` to `bcW.vacuum` and commutes with both restricted operators `a` and `aDagger`.

For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, if `bc.vacuum ‚Č† 0`, then there exists a `‚Ąā`-linear equivalence `ő¶ : Submodule.span ‚Ąā (Set.range bc.ket) ‚ČÉ‚āó[‚Ąā] (‚Ąē ‚Üí‚āÄ ‚Ąā)` such that for every `n : ‚Ąē`, `ő¶` sends the element of `Submodule.span ‚Ąā (Set.range bc.ket)` represented by `bc.ket n` to `Finsupp.single n 1`.
For every bosonic core `bc` with `bc.vacuum ‚Č† 0`, the linear map from `Polynomial ‚Ąā` to `V` given by `p ‚Ü¶ p(bc.aDagger) bc.vacuum` is injective.

For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, a vector `v : V` lies in `Submodule.span ‚Ąā (Set.range bc.ket)` if and only if there exists a polynomial `p : Polynomial ‚Ąā` such that `v = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum`.

For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, let `Phi : Polynomial ‚Ąā ‚Üí‚āó[‚Ąā] V` be the linear map defined by `Phi p = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum`. Then there exists a `‚Ąā`-linear equivalence `(Polynomial ‚Ąā ‚ßł LinearMap.ker Phi) ‚ČÉ‚āó[‚Ąā] Submodule.span ‚Ąā (Set.range bc.ket)`.

For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and endomorphism `T : Module.End ‚Ąā V`, `bc.a * (bc.aDagger * T) - (bc.aDagger * T) * bc.a = T + bc.aDagger * (bc.a * T - T * bc.a)`.
‚ąÄ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ‚Ąā V] (bc : BosonCore V) {v : V} {őľ : ‚Ąā} (n : ‚Ąē), bc.number v = őľ ‚ÄĘ v ‚Üí (bc.a ^ (n + 1)) v = 0 ‚Üí (bc.a ^ n) v ‚Č† 0 ‚Üí őľ = (n : ‚Ąā)
